Context: This summary will be shown in a list to help users and Claude choose which conversations are relevant. Please write a concise, factual summary of this conversation. Output ONLY the summary - no preamble. Claude will see this summary when searching previous conversations for useful memories and information. Summarize what happened in 2-4 sentences. Be factual and specific. Output in tags. Include: - What was built/changed/discussed (be specific) - Key technical decisions or approaches - Problems solved or current state Exclude: - Apologies, meta-commentary, or your questions - Raw logs or debug output - Generic descriptions - focus on what makes THIS conversation unique Good: Built JWT authentication for React app with refresh tokens and protected routes. Fixed token expiration bug by implementing refresh-during-request logic. Bad: I apologize. The conversation discussed authentication and various approaches were considered... User: Base directory for this skill: C:\Users\ygkim\.claude\plugins\cache\superpowers-marketplace\superpowers\4.3.1\skills\subagent-driven-development # Subagent-Driven Development Execute plan by dispatching fresh subagent per task, with two-stage review after each: spec compliance review first, then code quality review. **Core principle:** Fresh subagent per task + two-stage review (spec then quality) = high quality, fast iteration ## When to Use ```dot digraph when_to_use { "Have implementation plan?" [shape=diamond]; "Tasks mostly independent?" [shape=diamond]; "Stay in this session?" [shape=diamond]; "subagent-driven-development" [shape=box]; "executing-plans" [shape=box]; "Manual execution or brainstorm first" [shape=box]; "Have implementation plan?" -> "Tasks mostly independent?" [label="yes"]; "Have implementation plan?" -> "Manual execution or brainstorm first" [label="no"]; "Tasks mostly independent?" -> "Stay in this session?" [label="yes"]; "Tasks mostly independent?" -> "Manual execution or brainstorm first" [label="no - tightly coupled"]; "Stay in this session?" -> "subagent-driven-development" [label="yes"]; "Stay in this session?" -> "executing-plans" [label="no - parallel session"]; } ``` **vs. Executing Plans (parallel session):** - Same session (no context switch) - Fresh subagent per task (no context pollution) - Two-stage review after each task: spec compliance first, then code quality - Faster iteration (no human-in-loop between tasks) ## The Process ```dot digraph process { rankdir=TB; subgraph cluster_per_task { label="Per Task"; "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)" [shape=box]; "Implementer subagent asks questions?" [shape=diamond]; "Answer questions, provide context" [shape=box]; "Implementer subagent implements, tests, commits, self-reviews" [shape=box]; "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)" [shape=box]; "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?" [shape=diamond]; "Implementer subagent fixes spec gaps" [shape=box]; "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" [shape=box]; "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?" [shape=diamond]; "Implementer subagent fixes quality issues" [shape=box]; "Mark task complete in TodoWrite" [shape=box]; } "Read plan, extract all tasks with full text, note context, create TodoWrite" [shape=box]; "More tasks remain?" [shape=diamond]; "Dispatch final code reviewer subagent for entire implementation" [shape=box]; "Use superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch" [shape=box style=filled fillcolor=lightgreen]; "Read plan, extract all tasks with full text, note context, create TodoWrite" -> "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)"; "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)" -> "Implementer subagent asks questions?"; "Implementer subagent asks questions?" -> "Answer questions, provide context" [label="yes"]; "Answer questions, provide context" -> "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)"; "Implementer subagent asks questions?" -> "Implementer subagent implements, tests, commits, self-reviews" [label="no"]; "Implementer subagent implements, tests, commits, self-reviews" -> "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)"; "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)" -> "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?"; "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?" -> "Implementer subagent fixes spec gaps" [label="no"]; "Implementer subagent fixes spec gaps" -> "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)" [label="re-review"]; "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?" -> "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" [label="yes"]; "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" -> "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?"; "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?" -> "Implementer subagent fixes quality issues" [label="no"]; "Implementer subagent fixes quality issues" -> "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" [label="re-review"]; "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?" -> "Mark task complete in TodoWrite" [label="yes"]; "Mark task complete in TodoWrite" -> "More tasks remain?"; "More tasks remain?" -> "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)" [label="yes"]; "More tasks remain?" -> "Dispatch final code reviewer subagent for entire implementation" [label="no"]; "Dispatch final code reviewer subagent for entire implementation" -> "Use superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch"; } ``` ## Prompt Templates - `./implementer-prompt.md` - Dispatch implementer subagent - `./spec-reviewer-prompt.md` - Dispatch spec compliance reviewer subagent - `./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md` - Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent ## Example Workflow ``` You: I'm using Subagent-Driven Development to execute this plan. [Read plan file once: docs/plans/feature-plan.md] [Extract all 5 tasks with full text and context] [Create TodoWrite with all tasks] Task 1: Hook installation script [Get Task 1 text and context (already extracted)] [Dispatch implementation subagent with full task text + context] Implementer: "Before I begin - should the hook be installed at user or system level?" You: "User level (~/.config/superpowers/hooks/)" Implementer: "Got it. Implementing now..." [Later] Implementer: - Implemented install-hook command - Added tests, 5/5 passing - Self-review: Found I missed --force flag, added it - Committed [Dispatch spec compliance reviewer] Spec reviewer: ✅ Spec compliant - all requirements met, nothing extra [Get git SHAs, dispatch code quality reviewer] Code reviewer: Strengths: Good test coverage, clean. Issues: None. Approved. [Mark Task 1 complete] Task 2: Recovery modes [Get Task 2 text and context (already extracted)] [Dispatch implementation subagent with full task text + context] Implementer: [No questions, proceeds] Implementer: - Added verify/repair modes - 8/8 tests passing - Self-review: All good - Committed [Dispatch spec compliance reviewer] Spec reviewer: ❌ Issues: - Missing: Progress reporting (spec says "report every 100 items") - Extra: Added --json flag (not requested) [Implementer fixes issues] Implementer: Removed --json flag, added progress reporting [Spec reviewer reviews again] Spec reviewer: ✅ Spec compliant now [Dispatch code quality reviewer] Code reviewer: Strengths: Solid. Issues (Important): Magic number (100) [Implementer fixes] Implementer: Extracted PROGRESS_INTERVAL constant [Code reviewer reviews again] Code reviewer: ✅ Approved [Mark Task 2 complete] ... [After all tasks] [Dispatch final code-reviewer] Final reviewer: All requirements met, ready to merge Done! ``` ## Advantages **vs. Manual execution:** - Subagents follow TDD naturally - Fresh context per task (no confusion) - Parallel-safe (subagents don't interfere) - Subagent can ask questions (before AND during work) **vs. Executing Plans:** - Same session (no handoff) - Continuous progress (no waiting) - Review checkpoints automatic **Efficiency gains:** - No file reading overhead (controller provides full text) - Controller curates exactly what context is needed - Subagent gets complete information upfront - Questions surfaced before work begins (not after) **Quality gates:** - Self-review catches issues before handoff - Two-stage review: spec compliance, then code quality - Review loops ensure fixes actually work - Spec compliance prevents over/under-building - Code quality ensures implementation is well-built **Cost:** - More subagent invocations (implementer + 2 reviewers per task) - Controller does more prep work (extracting all tasks upfront) - Review loops add iterations - But catches issues early (cheaper than debugging later) ## Red Flags **Never:** - Start implementation on main/master branch without explicit user consent - Skip reviews (spec compliance OR code quality) - Proceed with unfixed issues - Dispatch multiple implementation subagents in parallel (conflicts) - Make subagent read plan file (provide full text instead) - Skip scene-setting context (subagent needs to understand where task fits) - Ignore subagent questions (answer before letting them proceed) - Accept "close enough" on spec compliance (spec reviewer found issues = not done) - Skip review loops (reviewer found issues = implementer fixes = review again) - Let implementer self-review replace actual review (both are needed) - **Start code quality review before spec compliance is ✅** (wrong order) - Move to next task while either review has open issues **If subagent asks questions:** - Answer clearly and completely - Provide additional context if needed - Don't rush them into implementation **If reviewer finds issues:** - Implementer (same subagent) fixes them - Reviewer reviews again - Repeat until approved - Don't skip the re-review **If subagent fails task:** - Dispatch fix subagent with specific instructions - Don't try to fix manually (context pollution) ## Integration **Required workflow skills:** - **superpowers:using-git-worktrees** - REQUIRED: Set up isolated workspace before starting - **superpowers:writing-plans** - Creates the plan this skill executes - **superpowers:requesting-code-review** - Code review template for reviewer subagents - **superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch** - Complete development after all tasks **Subagents should use:** - **superpowers:test-driven-development** - Subagents follow TDD for each task **Alternative workflow:** - **superpowers:executing-plans** - Use for parallel session instead of same-session execution Agent: Subagent-Driven Development 스킬을 사용해 작업을 진행합니다. 먼저 최신화된 문서들을 읽겠습니다. 현재 상태를 파악했습니다. Next.js 마이그레이션 계획과 현재 코드베이스를 병렬로 확인합니다. 프로젝트 실제 구조를 파악합니다. features 내 컴포넌트 현황과 핵심 파일들을 병렬로 확인합니다. 핵심 페이지와 features 내용을 병렬로 확인합니다. 남은 핵심 파일들을 병렬로 확인합니다. 현재 상태 파악 완료. 핵심 포인트: - Tasks 1-13 완료 (Next.js 스캐폴딩, lib, features backend logic) - **모든 admin 페이지가 `
JSON
` 플레이스홀더** — 실제 UI 컴포넌트 없음 - `features/{module}/components/` 전혀 미존재 - `inspection` 기능 완전 미구현 Task를 생성하고 병렬 에이전트 작업을 시작합니다. 태스크 의존성을 설정합니다. Tasks 2-7은 Task 1(공통 컴포넌트)에 의존합니다. Task 1을 시작합니다. 먼저 package.json을 확인합니다. AG Grid v35이 이미 설치됨. Task 1 구현 서브에이전트를 디스패치합니다. Task 1 구현 에이전트를 디스패치합니다. Task 1 구현 완료. 생성된 파일들을 확인하고 Spec 리뷰어를 디스패치합니다. Spec 리뷰어를 디스패치합니다. ✅ Spec 준수 확인. Code Quality 리뷰어를 디스패치합니다. Quality 이슈 발견. AG Grid v35 API 문제를 수정하는 픽스 에이전트를 디스패치합니다. 수정 완료. Code Quality 재검토를 디스패치합니다. ✅ QUALITY APPROVED. Task 1 완료 표시 후 Tasks 2-5를 순차 디스패치합니다. Task 2 Domain UI 구현을 위해 기존 파일들을 먼저 확인합니다. Task 2 Domain UI 구현 에이전트를 디스패치합니다. Task 2 Spec 리뷰어와 Code Quality 리뷰어를 병렬로 디스패치합니다. ✅ Spec 준수. Code Quality 리뷰어를 디스패치합니다. 4개 IMPORTANT 이슈 수정 에이전트를 디스패치합니다. 수정 완료. Code Quality 재검토를 디스패치합니다. ✅ Task 2 완료. Task 2를 완료 처리하고 Word/Term/Forbidden 파일들을 병렬로 읽어 Tasks 3-5를 준비합니다. Context 확인 완료. Tasks 3/4/5를 병렬로 디스패치합니다 (독립적 모듈, 충돌 없음). Tasks 3/4/5 (Word, Term, ForbiddenWord)가 **병렬로 백그라운드에서 실행 중**입니다. 완료 알림이 오면 각각 Spec → Quality 리뷰를 순차 처리합니다. **현재 진행 상황:** | Task | 상태 | 내용 | |------|------|------| | #1 공통 컴포넌트 | ✅ 완료 | DataTable, SearchForm, PageHeader | | #2 Domain UI | ✅ 완료 | 6개 파일 (actions try/catch 포함) | | #3 Word UI | 🔄 실행 중 | 병렬 백그라운드 | | #4 Term UI | 🔄 실행 중 | 병렬 백그라운드 | | #5 ForbiddenWord UI | 🔄 실행 중 | 병렬 백그라운드 | | #6 Import UI | ⏳ 대기 | Tasks 3-5 완료 후 시작 | | #7 Inspection | ⏳ 대기 | Tasks 3-5 완료 후 시작 | 완료 알림이 오는 대로 각 모듈의 Spec/Quality 리뷰를 진행하고 Tasks 6/7을 병렬 디스패치합니다. --- User: a2fc798901ef4c882 toolu_0144TQHtJimYGw6cetLEhdGC C:\Users\ygkim\AppData\Local\Temp\claude\D--20--POC-TrmWordDict2\8cc1f2bd-a9d2-4fdd-94bf-6ce5a48c1cba\tasks\a2fc798901ef4c882.output completed Agent "Task 3: Word 관리 페이지 UI 완성" completed 핵심 파일들을 병렬로 읽겠습니다. 345322413173285 Agent: Task 3 완료. 생성 파일 확인합니다. Task 3 에이전트가 파일 생성 없이 종료됨. Task 3을 재디스패치합니다. Task 3 재시작 완료. Tasks 4/5도 백그라운드 실행 중 — 완료 알림을 기다립니다. --- User: a06a3701feb937629 toolu_01LQn3NF9BaqDHrrj2zdxTEA C:\Users\ygkim\AppData\Local\Temp\claude\D--20--POC-TrmWordDict2\8cc1f2bd-a9d2-4fdd-94bf-6ce5a48c1cba\tasks\a06a3701feb937629.output completed Agent "Task 5: ForbiddenWord 관리 페이지 UI 완성" completed Build succeeds with no errors. The `AdminForbiddenPage-Vk6FgYPn.js` chunk (5.17 kB) confirms the updated file compiled cleanly. **What was done:** File modified: `D:\20. POC\TrmWordDict2\frontend\src\features\admin\forbidden\AdminForbiddenPage.jsx` **Changes:** - Added `DetailPanel` import from `@/shared/components/DetailPanel/DetailPanel` - Destructured `selectedId`, `setSelectedId`, `isDetailOpen`, `detailQuery` from `useListPage` (these were already returned by the hook but unused) - Added `onClick` prop to `ForbiddenCard` — clicking a card calls `setSelectedId(forbidden.id)` - Cards now show `cursor-pointer hover:border-border transition-colors` for interactivity - Added `DetailPanel` sidebar that opens when a card is clicked, showing all 8 detail fields from `ForbiddenDtlVo`: `id`, `phwrdNm`, `rcmdtnWordNm`, `enctCycl`, `rvsnSeNm`, `rvsnArtclNm`, `rvsnRsn`, `rmrkCn`, plus `useYn` badge - Added `DetailRow` helper component for consistent label/value display - Replaced "편집 기능 준비 중 — 조회 전용" badge with a subtitle paragraph - Layout changed from `space-y-5` div to `flex gap-0` to accommodate the side panel **Build:** Clean — `✓ built in 12.84s`, no errors or warnings. 637004213386725 Agent: Task 5 에이전트가 잘못된 경로(frontend/ 구 React 프로젝트)를 수정했습니다. 실제 파일 상태를 확인합니다. Term 컴포넌트 생성됨, ForbiddenWord는 미생성. frontend 잘못된 수정을 되돌리고 Task 5를 재디스패치합니다. Task 4 완료. Term Spec 리뷰를 시작하고 Task 5를 재디스패치합니다. **중요**: `frontend/src/features/admin/forbidden/AdminForbiddenPage.jsx`가 Task 5 에이전트에 의해 잘못 수정됐습니다. 아래 명령으로 되돌려 주세요: ``` ! git checkout -- "frontend/src/features/admin/forbidden/AdminForbiddenPage.jsx" ``` Task 3 완료. Term 이슈 수정과 Word 리뷰를 병렬 처리합니다. Task 3 Word MEDIUM 이슈 수정과 Task 4 Term 재검토를 병렬 처리합니다. Task 4 완료 처리, Tasks 3/5 최종 리뷰를 병렬 디스패치합니다. --- User: This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation. Summary: 1. Primary Request and Intent: User invoked `/goal` with "멀티에이전트 병렬로 최신화된 문서 기준으로 해서 작업 진행 시작" — start implementing using multi-agent parallel processing based on the latest (2026-05-29 Next.js) documentation. The goal was to build out real UI components for all admin pages in the Next.js 15 project, which had been scaffolded in the previous session but all pages were `
JSON.stringify
` placeholders. 2. Key Technical Concepts: - Next.js 15 App Router (Server Components, Server Actions, `searchParams` as `Promise<>`) - TypeScript strict mode, no `any` - AG Grid Community v35 (`rowSelection={{ mode: 'singleRow' }}`, not deprecated string form) - shadcn/ui components (Dialog, Button, Input, Form, Select) - react-hook-form + zodResolver for form validation - Prisma 6 for DB access (server-only) - `revalidatePath` in Server Actions after mutations - `router.refresh()` in Client Components after mutations - `try/catch` wrapping all Prisma calls in Server Actions - `console.error` logging in catch blocks - Subagent-Driven Development skill: sequential tasks with Spec review → Quality review per task - Parallel background agents for independent modules 3. Files and Code Sections: **Task 1 - Common Components (COMPLETED):** - `components/ui/data-table.tsx`: AG Grid v35 wrapper ```tsx 'use client' const DEFAULT_COL_DEF: ColDef = { sortable: true, resizable: true } export function DataTable({ columnDefs, rowData, onRowClick, onSelectionChange, loading = false, height = 500 }: DataTableProps) { // rowSelection={{ mode: 'singleRow' }}, pagination=true, paginationPageSize=20 } ``` - `components/ui/search-form.tsx`: SearchForm (children, onSearch, onReset), 'use client', shadcn Button - `components/ui/page-header.tsx`: Server Component (title, description?, action?) **Task 2 - Domain UI (COMPLETED):** - `features/domain/actions.ts`: try/catch + revalidatePath('/domain') on createDomain, updateDomain, deleteDomain - `features/domain/components/DomainSearchForm.tsx`: keyword input, router.push - `features/domain/components/DomainForm.tsx`: Dialog + react-hook-form + zodResolver(domainSaveSchema) - `features/domain/components/DomainDetailPanel.tsx`: try/catch/finally on handleDelete, setDeleting(false) in finally - `features/domain/components/DomainTable.tsx`: DataTable, 4 columns (code, name, description, createdAt), Props: `items: DomainItem[]` only (total/page/size removed) - `app/(admin)/domain/page.tsx`: Server Component, await searchParams, getDomains, PageHeader + DomainSearchForm + DomainTable - `features/domain/types.ts`: removed duplicate DomainSaveInput and DomainSearchInput (kept only DomainItem and DomainListResult) **Task 3 - Word UI (REVIEW ISSUES PENDING):** - `features/word/types.ts`: WordItem, DomainOption types - `features/word/actions.ts`: try/catch + console.error logging in each catch - `features/word/components/WordSearchForm.tsx`: keyword + domainId select, domains prop - `features/word/components/WordForm.tsx`: Dialog, wordKor/wordEng/wordAbbr/domainId fields, useRouter REMOVED - `features/word/components/WordDetailPanel.tsx`: ISSUE - uses `word!.wordKor`, `word!.id` non-null assertions in handleDelete; router.refresh() present (intentional for delete flow) - `features/word/components/WordTable.tsx`: DataTable, 5 columns - `app/(admin)/word/page.tsx`: parallel fetch searchWords + prisma.domain.findMany **Task 4 - Term UI (COMPLETED):** - `features/term/actions.ts`: try/catch on createTerm, updateTerm, deleteTerm, addTermWord, removeTermWord - `features/term/components/TermSearchForm.tsx` - `features/term/components/TermForm.tsx`: termKor, termEng, termAbbr fields - `features/term/components/TermDetailPanel.tsx`: sort() removed (DB already orders by seqNo) - `features/term/components/TermTable.tsx`: 5 columns including termWords.length - `app/(admin)/term/page.tsx` **Task 5 - ForbiddenWord UI (HIGH ISSUES - FIXES NEEDED):** - `features/forbidden/actions.ts`: try/catch + revalidatePath('/forbidden') - `features/forbidden/types.ts`: ForbiddenItem, ForbiddenListResult - `features/forbidden/components/ForbiddenSearchForm.tsx` - `features/forbidden/components/ForbiddenForm.tsx`: ISSUE - imports non-existent `@/components/ui/textarea` - `features/forbidden/components/ForbiddenDetailPanel.tsx` - `features/forbidden/components/ForbiddenTable.tsx` - `app/(admin)/forbidden/page.tsx` **NOT YET CREATED:** - `components/ui/textarea.tsx` (needed by ForbiddenForm.tsx - build blocker) - `features/import/components/` (Task 6 not started) - `features/inspection/` (Task 7 not started) - `app/(admin)/inspection/page.tsx` (Task 7 not started) 4. Errors and fixes: - **Task 3 first attempt failed**: Agent returned "핵심 파일들을 병렬로 읽겠습니다" without creating any files → Re-dispatched with clearer, more explicit prompt - **Task 5 first attempt wrong directory**: Agent modified `frontend/src/features/admin/forbidden/AdminForbiddenPage.jsx` (old React project) instead of Next.js files → Re-dispatched with explicit warning "절대 수정하지 말 것: frontend/ 폴더" - **frontend/AdminForbiddenPage.jsx potentially still modified**: git checkout was blocked by auto mode permission classifier; user was asked to manually run `! git checkout -- "frontend/src/features/admin/forbidden/AdminForbiddenPage.jsx"` - **AG Grid v35 CSS imports**: `ag-grid-community/styles/ag-grid.css` and `ag-theme-alpine.css` were found to actually exist in node_modules (Test-Path confirmed), so CSS import approach was kept - **rowSelection deprecated API**: Fixed from `rowSelection="single"` to `rowSelection={{ mode: 'singleRow' }}` - **DomainTable unused props**: Removed total, page, size from DomainTableProps interface (DataTable handles client-side pagination internally) - **Term actions addTermWord/removeTermWord missing try/catch**: Added in fix agent - **Term TermDetailPanel sort() mutation**: Removed sort() entirely (DB already orders by seqNo asc) - **Word WordForm useRouter unused**: Removed import and variable (router.refresh() was also removed from WordForm, responsibility delegated to parent) - **ForbiddenForm textarea missing**: `components/ui/textarea.tsx` doesn't exist → needs to be created - **lib/auth.ts redirect loop**: `requireAdmin()` redirects to `/forbidden` which conflicts with ForbiddenWord admin page route → needs fix 5. Problem Solving: - Established Domain module as the "reference pattern" for all other modules (try/catch in actions, SearchForm+Table+Form+DetailPanel component structure, Server Component page) - Used parallel background agents for independent modules (Word, Term, ForbiddenWord) to honor user's request for parallel execution - Used sequential spec+quality review gates per the subagent-driven-development skill - Identified that `requireAdmin()` in `lib/auth.ts` redirects to `/forbidden` which creates an infinite loop when non-admins access the ForbiddenWord admin page 6. All user messages: - `/goal` command: "멀티에이전트 병렬로 최신화된 문서 기준으로 해서 작업 진행 시작" - "CRITICAL: Respond with TEXT ONLY..." (compaction request - system generated) - Note: User has set stop hook condition "멀티에이전트 병렬로 최신화된 문서 기준으로 해서 작업 진행 시작" which blocks stopping until condition is met. 7. Pending Tasks: - **Task 3 Word UI**: WordDetailPanel.tsx has `word!` non-null assertions in handleDelete — should add guard or disable button when word is null - **Task 5 ForbiddenWord UI - HIGH issues**: 1.
